title: hydrobryum johnsonii
intro: Hydrobryum johnsonii, also known as Johnson's water moss, is a species of liverwort that belongs to the family Fabroniaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hydrobryum johnii include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Hydrobryum johnsonii is typically harvested and dried for use in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential.
